title: AI-Readiness Audit v1.4
$comment: Additive extension of v1.3. No breaking changes. New in v1.4: accessibility_intent_coverage and platform_readiness_gap dimension keys, sub_check_scores on DimensionEntry, phase_readiness_detail on Summary, AIC and PRG finding abbreviations. v1.3 consumers can still read v1.4 output by ignoring unknown optional fields.

meta.$comment: Provenance block. Everything needed to reproduce or trace the audit run. Kept separate from summary so CI systems can assert run identity without parsing results.

summary.$comment: Decision-making block. Consumed by stakeholders and CI gates. Contains no raw findings — only aggregated scores and ID references into the findings array. v1.4 adds phase_readiness_detail for remediation-path consumers.

dimensions.$comment: Keyed by dimension slug. Each entry holds a score, severity, narrative, and an array of finding IDs pointing into the top-level findings array. This indirection allows one finding to be referenced by more than one dimension. All dimension keys are optional at the property level. A partial audit omits unscored dimensions. summary.dimensions_scored records which were included. v1.4 adds platform_readiness_gap (replacing web_readiness_gap) and accessibility_intent_coverage.

web_readiness_gap.$comment: Deprecated in v1.4. Use platform_readiness_gap for new audits. Retained for backward compatibility with v1.3 audit outputs.

platform_readiness_gap.$comment: New in v1.4. Replaces web_readiness_gap. Platform-specific checks are configured in the client scoring config.

accessibility_intent_coverage.$comment: New in v1.4. Scores structural accessibility signals in the Figma file: focus states, touch targets, contrast derivability, keyboard navigation, accessibility mentions in descriptions.

findings.$comment: Flat canonical list. Dimensions reference finding IDs, not the other way around. Flat structure lets the codegen pipeline iterate all findings without traversing the dimension tree.

data_gaps.$comment: Explicit record of what could not be inspected. A consumer must not treat absence of findings as a clean result — data gaps explain the omissions.

config_ref.$comment: Pointer to the external weights config. Weights are never embedded in the audit output. This decouples client-specific weight adjustments from schema versioning.

version_delta.$comment: Optional. Present when a previous audit run exists. Records score changes and their causes. Stored in the audit output — not the config — because it is a factual record of measurement.

schema_version.$comment: Must match the $id version. Allows consumers to assert schema compatibility before parsing.

audit_id.$comment: Unique run identifier. Recommended format: {target}-v{version}-{date}, e.g. 'material-ui-v1.4-2026-04-01'. Referenced by version_delta.previous_audit_id in subsequent runs.

timestamp.$comment: ISO 8601 UTC. When the audit script completed, not when it started.

auditor.$comment: Who or what ran the audit, e.g. 'Claude Code via Figma MCP + REST API'.

script_version.$comment: Optional. Semantic version of the audit script. Lets you trace findings to a specific script revision when script and prompt versions diverge.

prompt_version.$comment: Optional. Version of the prompt template used. Separate from script_version because the prompt can change independently of the execution logic.

target_system.$comment: Name of the design system being audited, e.g. 'Material UI for Figma (Community)'.

figma_files.$comment: Keyed by library role (e.g. 'foundation', 'component'). Records the Figma file key and version at the time of the audit so results can be traced back to a specific file state.

file_name.$comment: Optional. Human-readable file name for the report.

overall_score.$comment: Weighted average of all scored dimensions, 0-100. Weights come from the file referenced by config_ref — not from this document. In v1.4, this is computed as sum of (dimension_score x weight) where dimension_score = average of sub-check scores x 25.

phase_readiness.$comment: Derived from overall_score and blocker count. 'pass' = score above threshold and zero blockers. 'conditional_pass' = warnings present but no blockers. 'not_ready' = one or more blocker-severity findings. Thresholds are defined in the weights config.

blocking_dimensions.$comment: Dimension slugs with severity=blocker. These must be resolved before the system can advance.

warning_dimensions.$comment: Dimension slugs with severity=warning. These degrade agent output quality but do not block advancement.

conditions_for_advancement.$comment: Explicit list of what must change for the system to advance from current phase_readiness. E.g. 'Resolve TA-001: create component-level token layer'. Each condition references a specific finding ID and describes the required action.

phase_readiness_detail.$comment: New in v1.4. Detail behind the phase_readiness enum. Consumers that only need pass/fail can ignore this. Consumers showing remediation paths use conditions_for_advancement.

top_blockers.$comment: Up to three finding IDs with severity=blocker, ordered by impact. These are the items that must be resolved before the system can advance. maxItems=3 enforces the editorial discipline of naming only the most critical issues.

dimension_scores.$comment: Flat {dimension_slug: score} lookup. Mirrors the score field inside each DimensionEntry. Provided here so dashboards and CI gates can read scores without traversing the full dimensions block.

dimensions_scored.$comment: Optional. Dimension slugs included in this run. Lets consumers detect partial audits — a slug absent here was not scored, not that it scored zero.

score.$comment: 0-100 scale. In v1.4, derived from sub-check scores: average of sub-checks x 25, rounded to nearest integer. v1.3 used implicit scoring without sub-checks.

severity.$comment: Dimension-level severity. In v1.4, derived from severity_thresholds in the scoring weights config AND the override rule: if any sub-check scores 0, severity is forced to blocker regardless of composite score. 'pass' is explicit — a dimension with no findings uses 'pass'.

narrative.$comment: Prose summary of this dimension's state. Used verbatim in the Markdown report. Should read as a standalone paragraph — it is the only dimension content that appears in the executive summary.

finding_ids.$comment: IDs pointing into the top-level findings array. Empty array is valid and means no issues found. A dimension with issues must list them here — the narrative alone is not sufficient for codegen consumers.

sub_check_scores.$comment: New in v1.4. Keyed by sub-check ID (e.g. '1a', '3c'). Each value is a 0-4 integer per the scale in the scoring weights config. The dimension score is derived from these via the formula: average x 25. Optional: omit for dimensions not yet using sub-check scoring or for v1.3-era audit outputs.

library_scope.$comment: Optional. Which library this score applies to. 'system' is an aggregate across libraries. 'cross_library' is used for findings that span both (e.g. text styles bypassing the variable architecture).

checks.$comment: Optional. Present only on dimensions that use programmatic pass/fail checks. Currently only 'governance' uses this. Not applicable to qualitative dimensions.

GovernanceCheck.$comment: A single programmatic check within a dimension. Modelled after the v1.2 governance scoring (5 checks, pass/fail). Extracted into its own definition so it can be reused if other dimensions add programmatic checks in future.

result.$comment: 'not_applicable' when a check cannot be run due to a data gap — not a failure, but not a pass either.

detail.$comment: Optional. Specific detail about the result, e.g. '10 of 33 pages have owner name suffixes'.

Finding.$comment: The atomic unit of the audit. Everything a codegen pipeline needs to know about one issue: what it is, where it is, how severe it is, and which contract field it relates to.

id.$comment: Stable unique identifier. Format: {DIMENSION_ABBREV}-{NNN}. Abbreviations: TI=token_implementation, AC=alias_chain_integrity, TA=token_architecture_depth, PN=primitive_naming, CTB=component_to_token_binding, CDC=component_description_coverage, NC=naming_convention_consistency, WR=web_readiness_gap (deprecated, use PRG), PRG=platform_readiness_gap, GOV=governance, DQ=documentation_quality, AIC=accessibility_intent_coverage. IDs must be stable across audit versions — if the same finding recurs in v1.5, it keeps the same ID.

dimension.$comment: Primary dimension this finding belongs to. Must match a key in the dimensions object. A finding that spans two dimensions lists the one where it is most severe as primary.

severity.$comment: 'blocker' = agent output will be wrong or missing; codegen cannot proceed. 'warning' = agent output will be inconsistent; human correction required on some outputs. 'note' = does not block agent use but reduces quality or maintainability.

node_id.$comment: Figma node ID of the affected element. Null for architectural findings with no single node (e.g. 'no component-level token layer exists').

node_name.$comment: Human-readable Figma node name. Null when node_id is null.

description.$comment: What the problem is. One to three sentences, written for a developer. Should be self-contained — a reader who has not read the dimension narrative can understand the issue from this field alone.

evidence.$comment: The observed data supporting the finding. Array when there are multiple observations. Verbatim where possible: 'Bounding box:fill=rgb(217,217,217) x18' is better than 'many hardcoded fills'.

recommendation.$comment: What to do to fix it. Actionable and specific. Should reference token names, file paths, or Figma operations where relevant. Not a restatement of the description.

contract_ref.$comment: Which contract field this finding relates to. This is the codegen hook — a pipeline joins findings to contracts via this field to know what to regenerate. Null for governance or naming findings with no direct contract reference.

affected_components.$comment: Optional. Component names affected by this finding. Required when the finding is cross-cutting. Omit for single-component findings where node_name is sufficient.

auto_fixable.$comment: Optional. True if a script could apply the fix without human judgment — e.g. rename a token, remove a trailing space. False or absent if the fix requires a design decision.

ContractRef.$comment: Points a finding at the specific contract field it relates to. Compatible with the Spain team's primitive/composite/pattern contract architecture at the level field.

type.$comment: 'token_definition' = finding is about a value or alias in the token files. 'component_contract' = finding relates to a prop, state, or token binding in a contract file. 'documentation_contract' = finding relates to a description, intent, whenToUse, or constraints field. 'governance_rule' = finding relates to a process or convention with no file equivalent.

level.$comment: Spain team architecture compatibility. 'primitive' = single-property token or foundational element. 'composite' = component with multiple tokens and states. 'pattern' = multi-component pattern or template. Null for governance_rule and documentation_contract types where level is not applicable.

path.$comment: Relative path from the repo root to the contract file, e.g. 'system/contracts/button.contract.json'. Null when the contract does not exist yet — the finding implies one should be created.

field.$comment: Dot-notation path to the specific field within the contract, e.g. 'tokens.color.byState.Disabled.background'. Null when the finding applies to the whole contract rather than a specific field.

DataGap.$comment: A named gap in audit coverage. Each gap has an ID, a reason enum, and an impact field so consumers know whether a gap likely inflates or deflates a score.

id.$comment: Unique gap ID, e.g. 'GAP-001'. Stable across runs if the same gap recurs.

description.$comment: What could not be inspected and why.

reason.$comment: 'timeout' = MCP timed out. 'access_denied' = API or permission restriction. 'scope_excluded' = deliberately out of scope for this audit version. 'not_auditable' = structurally unreadable by the tool. 'page_size' = page has too many nodes for the MCP to traverse.

affected_components.$comment: Optional. Component or node names that fall inside this gap.

impact.$comment: Optional. Whether the gap likely inflates or deflates a score, and why.

path.$comment: Relative path from the repo root to the weights config, e.g. 'config/scoring-weights_v1.4.json'. Weights are not embedded in the audit output — they are per-client and change independently of the schema version.

version.$comment: Optional. Version of the weights config active when this audit ran. Useful when multiple clients use different weight profiles.

previous_audit_id.$comment: The audit_id of the previous run. Used to trace score changes to a specific prior output file.

previous_score.$comment: String to accommodate 'not_scored' for dimensions that are new in this version.

dimensions_changed.$comment: Only dimensions where the score changed. Unchanged dimensions are omitted to keep the delta compact.

dimensions_added.$comment: Dimension slugs that are new in this audit version. These will always appear in dimensions_changed with previous_score='not_scored'.

narrative.$comment: Optional. Prose summary of what changed and why. Used verbatim in the Markdown version delta section.
